items.quote: When I was young, I was just about hard work. But as I got older, I did experience anxiety, doubt, judgment, and it's so easy to lose yourself for a second.
items.author: Jennifer Lopez

items.title: MARCH: Multi-Agent Radiology Clinical Hierarchy for CT Report Generation
items.abstract: MARCH is a multi-agent framework for CT report generation that mimics the hierarchical structure of radiology departments to improve clinical fidelity. It employs specialized agents—Resident for drafting, Fellows for retrieval-augmented revision, and an Attending for orchestrating consensus discourse—to iteratively verify findings. This approach effectively mitigates clinical hallucinations and outperforms existing monolithic models on the RadGenome-ChestCT dataset.
items.figure_caption: Overview of the MARCH framework. It consists of three main stages: 1) Initial Report Drafting, 2) Retrieval-Augmented Report Revision, and 3) Consensus-Driven Finalization.

items.title: UniEditBench: A Unified and Cost-Effective Benchmark for Image and Video Editing via Distilled MLLMs
items.abstract: UniEditBench introduces a unified and cost-effective benchmark for evaluating both image and video editing models using a shared protocol, addressing the fragmentation in current evaluation methods. Its core contribution is providing a structured taxonomy of diverse editing operations and enabling scalable assessment by distilling a powerful MLLM judge into a lightweight, efficient evaluator. This allows for fairer cross-paradigm comparisons without incurring prohibitive computational costs.
items.figure_caption: Figure 1. Overview of the UniEditBench framework. (A) Dataset Composition & Detailed Taxonomy: Displays the structured task taxonomy and data distribution, comprising 633 image and 77 video samples across diverse editing operations (e.g., content modification, attribute transformation, and compositional reasoning tasks like counting and reordering). (B) Evaluation Pipeline with Expert Distillation: Our pipeline provides a source prompt, target prompt, and edited prompt for each sample pair to accommodate various editing paradigms. Additionally, the editing results, original images, and prompt information are input into our 4B/8B discriminative models, which are distilled from the Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B model, to obtain the final judgments. (C) Comprehensive Benchmark Results: Radar charts display the aggregated evaluation results of editing methods on UniEditBench, assessed by the distilled 4B and 8B reward models (top: image benchmark; bottom: video benchmark).

items.title: Do Vision-Language Models Truly Perform Vision Reasoning? A Rigorous Study of the Modality Gap
items.abstract: This paper rigorously investigates whether Vision-Language Models (VLMs) perform genuine vision-grounded reasoning or rely on their text-only capabilities. The core method introduces **CrossMath**, a novel benchmark where problems are presented in text-only, image-only, and combined formats with strictly identical information content. The contribution is demonstrating a consistent and substantial performance gap, showing VLMs significantly outperform on text-only inputs compared to image-based reasoning, suggesting limitations in their visual grounding.

items.title: Mind's Eye: A Benchmark of Visual Abstraction, Transformation and Composition for Multimodal LLMs
items.abstract: This paper introduces "Mind's Eye," a novel benchmark designed to assess the visual cognitive and visuospatial reasoning abilities of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) through eight tasks categorized by Abstraction, Relation, and Transformation. The benchmark reveals a significant gap, with top MLLMs scoring below 50% accuracy compared to 80% for humans. This highlights current MLLMs' limitations in crucial fluid intelligence processes like pattern induction and mental manipulation.
items.figure_caption: Overview of the eight tasks in the proposed Mind’s Eye benchmark : Each panel shows an example image-question pair of the benchmark

items.title: Information Router for Mitigating Modality Dominance in Vision-Language Models
items.abstract: This paper introduces the **Multi-modal Information Router ($\text{MoIR}$)** to combat modality dominance in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) by addressing information disparity *before* fusion. $\text{MoIR}$ explicitly identifies tokens with lower information density and routes complementary information from a stronger modality to them. This information-level routing mechanism ensures a more balanced and robust fusion process compared to methods that only adjust attention allocation.

items.title: Prototype-Grounded Concept Models for Verifiable Concept Alignment
items.abstract: This paper introduces Prototype-Grounded Concept Models (PGCMs) to address the lack of verifiability in Concept Bottleneck Models (CBMs). PGCMs ground abstract concepts in learned visual prototypes, providing explicit image evidence for concept activation. This grounding allows for direct inspection and targeted human intervention to correct concept misalignments, achieving comparable predictive performance to CBMs while significantly enhancing transparency and interpretability.
items.figure_caption: Comparison between standard neural networks, Concept Bottleneck Models (CBMs), and our Prototype-Grounded Concept Models (PGCMs). While CBMs explain predictions in terms of high-level concepts, they provide no way to verify what those concepts mean visually. PGCMs ground each concept in learned visual prototypes, making concept semantics explicit and inspectable.

items.title: Robust Multispectral Semantic Segmentation under Missing or Full Modalities via Structured Latent Projection
items.abstract: This paper introduces CBC-SLP, a novel method for robust multispectral semantic segmentation that handles both missing and full modalities. The core method employs a **Structured Latent Projection (SLP)** to explicitly learn and preserve both modality-invariant and modality-specific information, avoiding the performance trade-off seen in models relying solely on shared representations. This approach ensures high performance even when all modalities are available while maintaining robustness to missing data.
items.figure_caption: Multispectral optical (false RGB: infrared-red-green channels) image on the left and DEM height map on the right, both from the Hunan image set [ 17 ] . While the multispectral image highlights surface reflectance and land-cover differences, the DEM height map reveals the terrain structure, providing complementary information for scene interpretation.

items.title: GitHub's fake star economy
items.summary: An investigation revealed a thriving economy on GitHub involving the buying and selling of fake repository stars, often facilitated by automated bots.

items.title: Qwen3.6-Max-Preview: Smarter, Sharper, Still Evolving
items.summary: Alibaba has previewed Qwen3.6-Max, a new iteration of its large language model that promises enhanced intelligence and performance while remaining under active development.

items.title: Atlassian enables default data collection to train AI
items.summary: Atlassian has enabled data collection by default to train its artificial intelligence models, impacting user data usage.

items.title: AI Resistance: some recent anti-AI stuff that’s worth discussing
items.summary: The tech news story discusses the growing resistance to Artificial Intelligence, highlighting recent anti-AI developments and arguments that warrant further consideration.

items.title: OpenClaw isn't fooling me. I remember MS-DOS
items.summary: The article details how to build an OpenClaw local AI agent, emphasizing its free, secure, and always-on nature, drawing a nostalgic comparison to MS-DOS.

items.title: Deezer says 44% of songs uploaded to its platform daily are AI-generated
items.summary: Deezer reports that nearly half of the music uploaded to its platform each day is now being generated by artificial intelligence.

items.title: I prompted ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, and Gemini and watched my Nginx logs
items.summary: The author analyzed Nginx logs to compare the traffic patterns generated by interactions with various AI models (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, Gemini) against traditional referral traffic.

items.description: FinceptTerminal is a modern finance application offering advanced market analytics, investment research, and economic data tools, designed for interactive exploration and data-driven decision-making in a user-friendly environment.
items.summary: FinceptTerminal is a modern finance application providing advanced market analytics, investment research, and economic data tools for interactive exploration and data-driven decision-making.

items.description: π RuView: WiFi DensePose turns commodity WiFi signals into real-time human pose estimation, vital sign monitoring, and presence detection — all without a single pixel of video.
items.summary: RuView is a system that uses commodity WiFi signals to perform real-time human pose estimation, vital sign monitoring, and presence detection without requiring any video input.

items.description: AI You Control: Choose your models. Own your data. Eliminate vendor lock-in.
items.summary: Thunderbolt is an AI platform enabling users to control their models, own their data, and avoid vendor lock-in by offering model choice and data sovereignty.

items.description: A community-supported supercharged document management system: scan, index and archive all your documents
items.summary: Paperless-ngx is a community-supported document management system that allows users to scan, index, and archive all their documents efficiently.

items.description: Enterprise Architecture Governance & Vendor Procurement Toolkit
items.summary: Arc-Kit is an enterprise architecture governance and vendor procurement toolkit designed to streamline and manage architectural compliance and the acquisition of necessary technologies.

items.description: Real-time global intelligence dashboard. AI-powered news aggregation, geopolitical monitoring, and infrastructure tracking in a unified situational awareness interface
items.summary: WorldMonitor is a real-time global intelligence dashboard that uses AI to aggregate news, monitor geopolitics, and track infrastructure within a unified situational awareness interface.

items.description: A lightweight, powerful framework for multi-agent workflows
items.summary: The `openai-agents-python` repository provides a lightweight, powerful framework for building and managing complex multi-agent workflows using Python.

items.description: DeepGEMM: clean and efficient FP8 GEMM kernels with fine-grained scaling
items.summary: DeepGEMM provides clean and efficient FP8 General Matrix Multiplication (GEMM) kernels featuring fine-grained scaling for optimized performance.

items.description: A black hole for Internet advertisements
items.summary: Pi-hole functions as a network-level advertisement blocker, acting as a DNS sinkhole to filter ads and trackers for all connected devices.
